title: inula fragilis
intro: Inula fragilis, also known as St. James' Wort or Naked-Stemmed Inula, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family with yellow daisy-like flowers and a unique, almost hairless stem. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the sesquiterpene lactones present in its roots. The health benefits of Inula fragilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin conditions and digestive issues. Inula fragilis is often prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including eczema, acne, and gastrointestinal problems.
